features.evidence: when you re looking at high end productivity laptops the two models that most people go for are the apple macbook pro 16 and the dell xps 17 because those are the laptops that offer some of the best specs on the market combined with the best build quality and they also carry a pretty high end price tag to match that but there are also other laptops on the market that are worth checking out too and today i m going to talk about this creator z17 which is a model that msi made to fit this top tier segment as well so it is made of metal it is pretty thin but it still holds the fastest hardware the money can buy and yes you will need a lot of money for this one as well so let s see how it performs and if it s worth getting at all let s begin 5 kilos without the charger on the left side we have the power connector two thunderbolt ports with one of them allowing usb charging as well and there is a headphone jack and on the right side there is an sd card reader a 10 gigabit usb type a port and an hdmi 2

1 port that supports 4k 120 or 8k 60 monitors now ideally it would have been nice to see a wired network connection but other than that i would say it s a pretty reasonable setup now it is very easy to open it up with one hand and if you go beyond 100 degrees or so it lifts up the bottom of the laptop which gives it a bit of an extra airflow now the build quality on the inside is pretty good too there s a bit of a flex when pressing down on it but nothing that would really affect your typing or your workflow they went with a typical tactile keyboard that they use on most of their gaming laptops it is nice to work on it and it is very easy to get used to it as well it has an rgb backlight which would be great for a gaming laptop but it doesn t really match an elegant creator laptop at least in my opinion i guess it s good to have an option to set it to any single color that you but personally i would have been fine with it just being white when it comes to the touchpad it is completely fine as well it is pretty large it is pretty smooth and it has an excellent wrist rejection which is very important because most of the time your right hand will cover it while you re typing but let s see that performance every thin high performance laptop needs to find a good balance between performance thermals and noise which is not that easy when you have a high power i9 12900h in a small chassis this one and every manufacturer has a different way of finding this balance now what msi did here is that they let the chip use quite a lot of power and run quite hot for a short while before tuning down the power of the cpu so in performance profile the cpu will use about 115 watts once you set it to work and hit 96 to 97 degrees but after a minute or so it drops down to 70 watts and it cools down so if we look at the benchmarks that don t take too long to complete the z17 is all the way up there with the fastest laptops on the market but if a task takes a little bit longer the 10 minute cinebench r23 benchmark for example it will drop down in the charts now keep in mind that even at 70 watts the i 912 900h is still one of the fastest cpus in the market and it will handle pretty much anything really well but it also means that it s better suited for tasks that require a lot of cpu power for a short while so if you are someone that spends a ton of time exporting long video projects you might want to reconsider if going thinner is worth it for you or not now during longer workloads the cpu stays at acceptable temperatures but it is a bit noisy standing at almost 47 decibels now if you want even better thermals msi offers a cooler boost setting but that makes the laptop so obnoxiously loud that i just cannot really recommend that unless you re really sitting alone and using a good headset if you want something quieter there is a balance mode as well which drops to a very nice and comfortable 35

when you re looking at high end productivity laptops the two models that most people go for are the apple macbook pro 16 and the dell xps 17 because those are the laptops that offer some of the best specs on the market combined with the best build quality and they also carry a pretty high end price tag to match that but there are also other laptops on the market that are worth checking out too and today i m going to talk about this creator z17 which is a model that msi made to fit this top tier segment as well so it is made of metal it is pretty thin but it still holds the fastest hardware the money can buy and yes you will need a lot of money for this one as well so let s see how it performs and if it s worth getting at all let s begin 5 kilos without the charger on the left side we have the power connector two thunderbolt ports with one of them allowing usb charging as well and there is a headphone jack and on the right side there is an sd card reader a 10 gigabit usb type a port and an hdmi 2

4 decibels under load while still showing good temps now the cpa power does drop to 35 watts which hurts the performance a bit more still you do have two usable and completely different profiles to choose from depending on what you re doing if we look at gaming or other workloads where the gpu uses a lot of power cooling becomes even harder to balance now exact cpu and gpu power levels will vary per game and per application but if we look at cyberpunk for example we can see temps just under 90 degrees while the gpu is using around 75 watts of power and the cpu around 35 watts now the fans are definitely audible at around 46 decibels again but it is manageable now i just think the balance mode is worth using if you care about gpu performance it does make the laptop quieter but it drops the gpu power to about 58 watts which again really hurts the performance if we look at the specs the gpu is supposed to boost up to 90 watts and even though it hits that number sometimes most of the time it is around that 75 watt mark and that puts this creator z17 in a tough spot compared to thicker laptops with much higher power limits if we compare it to the 115 watt scar 15 for example that comes with a 30 70 ti this car will be a lot faster and i do know that this is not a gaming laptop and that it will still easily outperform other thin and light productivity laptops the xps 17 for example with a low power 3060 but i also think that it is fair to mention that even though you spend a lot of money to buy an rtx 3080 ti laptop to get the best performance a lower spec laptop that has higher power limits will do better in gaming as well as non gaming tasks so do keep this in mind now the creator 17 comes with a 2560 by 1600p display that runs at 165 hertz which is also a bit confusing for a productivity laptop because they decided to go for a higher refresh rate instead of going for a higher resolution and there are no 4k options available at all it only comes with this quad hd plus display and while its higher refresh rate will make it feel smoother than a 60hz display and a bit more vertical pixels is nice to have a 4k resolution screen will be sharper and it will offer more workspace which in my opinion would fit this laptop a bit more it is a touch screen panel and it is compatible with the msi pen but unfortunately the pen is optional so in some regions as stores it will be included but not always so my unit for example didn t come with one so i cannot really tell you how it works but if you want to buy the pen yourself it will cost you around 100 euros or around 130 dollars in the us now the fact that it is touchscreen does make it more reflective than most non touch laptops and that can look very pretty with colors popping just a bit more but it can also be very annoying if you re using it in a very bright environment because it will just reflect everything now in other areas the display is fine but it s also not amazing so the brightness caps out at 383 nits which is more than enough for indoor use but it is a bit low for proper outdoor use the minimum white level is 8 in it so it does dim nicely if that is important to you now contrast is somewhat disappointing standing at 935 to 1 and the color performance is good but it is not top tier you get a proper srdb coverage and about 92 percent of p3 gamut coverage the same goes for the factory calibration so most colors are calibrated well but there are a few hiccups here and there now the gray balance is off by a lot for example so if you re really critical about color accuracy and gray accuracy you do have to calibrate it yourself now the z17 has a 90 watt hour battery which gives it a reasonable battery life in the pc mark 8 test it lasted about 3 hours and 45 minutes and in the pc mark 10 modern office benchmark around five and a half hours so it s not bad but it s also not an all day a laptop you can stretch it up to about six hours while watching netflix but don t expect to game or work with the gpu because you ll be out of the battery in about an hour and a half the speakers are decent they have a pretty nice sound quality but they don t go too loud and the webcam is definitely good enough for some meetings but it is not spectacular okay so this is the test of the camera and of the microphone on the creator z17 from msi looks pretty decent bye now since this laptop is so thin there isn t that much you can do when it comes to maintenance and upgradeability most of the motherboard is taped over with insulating tape that is hard to remove and to reapply and you won t be able to upgrade the memory but they did make it easy enough to clean the fans to change the wifi chip to change the battery and to add a second ssd to it but there are a few things that really sit well with me and the first thing is that even though the cpu and gpu thermals look okay some parts of the laptop itself do get pretty hot so while working in premiere or while playing a game for example the part that is above the keyboard as well as the bottom of the display itself easily hit around 55 degrees which really feels hot and it is a touch screen so touching is implied also the area left of the keyboard gets hot as well where all the connections are so you do touch it when plugging devices in and out and it is pretty hot to touch it so please keep this in mind and the second thing is the software experience now i really dislike the fact that data collection is on by default without any notice and you need to look for some tiny font in the software to be able to turn that off this should be 100 opt in and not out and i don t even think this is allowed under privacy laws here in the eu so msi might want to look a bit more into that and they also should check their spelling and grammar because it just looks so unprofessional from the could you to when turning off data collection to the confusing double negative auto update prompt they re just a bunch of mistakes that should not happen on a laptop of this caliber especially when the competition in this segment pays a lot of attention to detail but let s look at the price now there are several configurations that you can choose from and the most basic one with an i7 a 37 eti and 32 gigs of ram will cost you around three thousand dollars without taxes in the us or around three thousand euros here in europe and from there on it goes up to forty five hundred dollars or almost five thousand euros for the top spec model that comes with an i9 a 3080 ti and 64 gigs of ram which is the model that i m testing today but the problem is that for that amount of money you just have way too many alternatives out there for about 2 500 you can buy a gaming laptop that will offer a better cpu and better gpu performance with a similar panel quality while being only about 500 grams heavier or you can save about 1 000 and go for a top spec xps 17 that has a slower gpu but a better display or you can go for a macbook pro 16 and still have about two hundred dollars left over so this top spec z17 is just too expensive in my opinion now the three thousand dollar model with an i7 and a 30 70 ti is a bit more interesting in my opinion it s a lot cheaper it has similar power levels to this one so the performance will not be that far off and to me it just makes so much more sense because if you compare that one to the xps you re suddenly getting a lot more gpu power in a nice and thin workstation laptop and that is the main issue with these laptops that want the highest possible spec in a thinner form factor they want to have the strongest cpu and the strongest gpu options in under two centimeters but then they need to limit their power just to make it work and on paper it seems you re paying this super premium price for a top performing laptop while the lower spec options with more power will cost you less and they will perform just as well if not even better and i don t really think that msi could have found a much better balance here between performance thermals and noise without making it a bit thicker but that doesn t change the fact that there are some clear consequences if you choose to go this way so personally if going this thin is super important to you the z17 could make a lot of sense if you don t go for the top spec model but if you re okay with going slightly thicker there are other options on the market that will cost you less they will run significantly faster and they will have an easier time managing thermals and noise so you need to figure out for yourself what is important to you before making this choice now that is all i have for today thank you all for watching this video and see you guys in the next one bye you

when you re looking at high end productivity laptops the two models that most people go for are the apple macbook pro 16 and the dell xps 17 because those are the laptops that offer some of the best specs on the market combined with the best build quality and they also carry a pretty high end price tag to match that but there are also other laptops on the market that are worth checking out too and today i m going to talk about this creator z17 which is a model that msi made to fit this top tier segment as well so it is made of metal it is pretty thin but it still holds the fastest hardware the money can buy and yes you will need a lot of money for this one as well so let s see how it performs and if it s worth getting at all let s begin 5 kilos without the charger on the left side we have the power connector two thunderbolt ports with one of them allowing usb charging as well and there is a headphone jack and on the right side there is an sd card reader a 10 gigabit usb type a port and an hdmi 2

when you re looking at high end productivity laptops the two models that most people go for are the apple macbook pro 16 and the dell xps 17 because those are the laptops that offer some of the best specs on the market combined with the best build quality and they also carry a pretty high end price tag to match that but there are also other laptops on the market that are worth checking out too and today i m going to talk about this creator z17 which is a model that msi made to fit this top tier segment as well so it is made of metal it is pretty thin but it still holds the fastest hardware the money can buy and yes you will need a lot of money for this one as well so let s see how it performs and if it s worth getting at all let s begin 5 kilos without the charger on the left side we have the power connector two thunderbolt ports with one of them allowing usb charging as well and there is a headphone jack and on the right side there is an sd card reader a 10 gigabit usb type a port and an hdmi 2

4 decibels under load while still showing good temps now the cpa power does drop to 35 watts which hurts the performance a bit more still you do have two usable and completely different profiles to choose from depending on what you re doing if we look at gaming or other workloads where the gpu uses a lot of power cooling becomes even harder to balance now exact cpu and gpu power levels will vary per game and per application but if we look at cyberpunk for example we can see temps just under 90 degrees while the gpu is using around 75 watts of power and the cpu around 35 watts now the fans are definitely audible at around 46 decibels again but it is manageable now i just think the balance mode is worth using if you care about gpu performance it does make the laptop quieter but it drops the gpu power to about 58 watts which again really hurts the performance if we look at the specs the gpu is supposed to boost up to 90 watts and even though it hits that number sometimes most of the time it is around that 75 watt mark and that puts this creator z17 in a tough spot compared to thicker laptops with much higher power limits if we compare it to the 115 watt scar 15 for example that comes with a 30 70 ti this car will be a lot faster and i do know that this is not a gaming laptop and that it will still easily outperform other thin and light productivity laptops the xps 17 for example with a low power 3060 but i also think that it is fair to mention that even though you spend a lot of money to buy an rtx 3080 ti laptop to get the best performance a lower spec laptop that has higher power limits will do better in gaming as well as non gaming tasks so do keep this in mind now the creator 17 comes with a 2560 by 1600p display that runs at 165 hertz which is also a bit confusing for a productivity laptop because they decided to go for a higher refresh rate instead of going for a higher resolution and there are no 4k options available at all it only comes with this quad hd plus display and while its higher refresh rate will make it feel smoother than a 60hz display and a bit more vertical pixels is nice to have a 4k resolution screen will be sharper and it will offer more workspace which in my opinion would fit this laptop a bit more it is a touch screen panel and it is compatible with the msi pen but unfortunately the pen is optional so in some regions as stores it will be included but not always so my unit for example didn t come with one so i cannot really tell you how it works but if you want to buy the pen yourself it will cost you around 100 euros or around 130 dollars in the us now the fact that it is touchscreen does make it more reflective than most non touch laptops and that can look very pretty with colors popping just a bit more but it can also be very annoying if you re using it in a very bright environment because it will just reflect everything now in other areas the display is fine but it s also not amazing so the brightness caps out at 383 nits which is more than enough for indoor use but it is a bit low for proper outdoor use the minimum white level is 8 in it so it does dim nicely if that is important to you now contrast is somewhat disappointing standing at 935 to 1 and the color performance is good but it is not top tier you get a proper srdb coverage and about 92 percent of p3 gamut coverage the same goes for the factory calibration so most colors are calibrated well but there are a few hiccups here and there now the gray balance is off by a lot for example so if you re really critical about color accuracy and gray accuracy you do have to calibrate it yourself now the z17 has a 90 watt hour battery which gives it a reasonable battery life in the pc mark 8 test it lasted about 3 hours and 45 minutes and in the pc mark 10 modern office benchmark around five and a half hours so it s not bad but it s also not an all day a laptop you can stretch it up to about six hours while watching netflix but don t expect to game or work with the gpu because you ll be out of the battery in about an hour and a half the speakers are decent they have a pretty nice sound quality but they don t go too loud and the webcam is definitely good enough for some meetings but it is not spectacular okay so this is the test of the camera and of the microphone on the creator z17 from msi looks pretty decent bye now since this laptop is so thin there isn t that much you can do when it comes to maintenance and upgradeability most of the motherboard is taped over with insulating tape that is hard to remove and to reapply and you won t be able to upgrade the memory but they did make it easy enough to clean the fans to change the wifi chip to change the battery and to add a second ssd to it but there are a few things that really sit well with me and the first thing is that even though the cpu and gpu thermals look okay some parts of the laptop itself do get pretty hot so while working in premiere or while playing a game for example the part that is above the keyboard as well as the bottom of the display itself easily hit around 55 degrees which really feels hot and it is a touch screen so touching is implied also the area left of the keyboard gets hot as well where all the connections are so you do touch it when plugging devices in and out and it is pretty hot to touch it so please keep this in mind and the second thing is the software experience now i really dislike the fact that data collection is on by default without any notice and you need to look for some tiny font in the software to be able to turn that off this should be 100 opt in and not out and i don t even think this is allowed under privacy laws here in the eu so msi might want to look a bit more into that and they also should check their spelling and grammar because it just looks so unprofessional from the could you to when turning off data collection to the confusing double negative auto update prompt they re just a bunch of mistakes that should not happen on a laptop of this caliber especially when the competition in this segment pays a lot of attention to detail but let s look at the price now there are several configurations that you can choose from and the most basic one with an i7 a 37 eti and 32 gigs of ram will cost you around three thousand dollars without taxes in the us or around three thousand euros here in europe and from there on it goes up to forty five hundred dollars or almost five thousand euros for the top spec model that comes with an i9 a 3080 ti and 64 gigs of ram which is the model that i m testing today but the problem is that for that amount of money you just have way too many alternatives out there for about 2 500 you can buy a gaming laptop that will offer a better cpu and better gpu performance with a similar panel quality while being only about 500 grams heavier or you can save about 1 000 and go for a top spec xps 17 that has a slower gpu but a better display or you can go for a macbook pro 16 and still have about two hundred dollars left over so this top spec z17 is just too expensive in my opinion now the three thousand dollar model with an i7 and a 30 70 ti is a bit more interesting in my opinion it s a lot cheaper it has similar power levels to this one so the performance will not be that far off and to me it just makes so much more sense because if you compare that one to the xps you re suddenly getting a lot more gpu power in a nice and thin workstation laptop and that is the main issue with these laptops that want the highest possible spec in a thinner form factor they want to have the strongest cpu and the strongest gpu options in under two centimeters but then they need to limit their power just to make it work and on paper it seems you re paying this super premium price for a top performing laptop while the lower spec options with more power will cost you less and they will perform just as well if not even better and i don t really think that msi could have found a much better balance here between performance thermals and noise without making it a bit thicker but that doesn t change the fact that there are some clear consequences if you choose to go this way so personally if going this thin is super important to you the z17 could make a lot of sense if you don t go for the top spec model but if you re okay with going slightly thicker there are other options on the market that will cost you less they will run significantly faster and they will have an easier time managing thermals and noise so you need to figure out for yourself what is important to you before making this choice now that is all i have for today thank you all for watching this video and see you guys in the next one bye you

also the area left of the keyboard gets hot as well where all the connections are so you do touch it when plugging devices in and out and it is pretty hot to touch it so please keep this in mind and the second thing is the software experience now i really dislike the fact that data collection is on by default without any notice and you need to look for some tiny font in the software to be able to turn that off this should be 100 opt in and not out and i don t even think this is allowed under privacy laws here in the eu so msi might want to look a bit more into that and they also should check their spelling and grammar because it just looks so unprofessional from the could you to when turning off data collection to the confusing double negative auto update prompt they re just a bunch of mistakes that should not happen on a laptop of this caliber especially when the competition in this segment pays a lot of attention to detail but let s look at the price now there are several configurations that you can choose from and the most basic one with an i7 a 37 eti and 32 gigs of ram will cost you around three thousand dollars without taxes in the us or around three thousand euros here in europe and from there on it goes up to forty five hundred dollars or almost five thousand euros for the top spec model that comes with an i9 a 3080 ti and 64 gigs of ram which is the model that i m testing today but the problem is that for that amount of money you just have way too many alternatives out there for about 2 500 you can buy a gaming laptop that will offer a better cpu and better gpu performance with a similar panel quality while being only about 500 grams heavier or you can save about 1 000 and go for a top spec xps 17 that has a slower gpu but a better display or you can go for a macbook pro 16 and still have about two hundred dollars left over so this top spec z17 is just too expensive in my opinion now the three thousand dollar model with an i7 and a 30 70 ti is a bit more interesting in my opinion it s a lot cheaper it has similar power levels to this one so the performance will not be that far off and to me it just makes so much more sense because if you compare that one to the xps you re suddenly getting a lot more gpu power in a nice and thin workstation laptop and that is the main issue with these laptops that want the highest possible spec in a thinner form factor they want to have the strongest cpu and the strongest gpu options in under two centimeters but then they need to limit their power just to make it work and on paper it seems you re paying this super premium price for a top performing laptop while the lower spec options with more power will cost you less and they will perform just as well if not even better and i don t really think that msi could have found a much better balance here between performance thermals and noise without making it a bit thicker but that doesn t change the fact that there are some clear consequences if you choose to go this way so personally if going this thin is super important to you the z17 could make a lot of sense if you don t go for the top spec model but if you re okay with going slightly thicker there are other options on the market that will cost you less they will run significantly faster and they will have an easier time managing thermals and noise so you need to figure out for yourself what is important to you before making this choice now that is all i have for today thank you all for watching this video and see you guys in the next one bye you

there s a bit of a flex when pressing down on it but nothing that would really affect your typing or your workflow they went with a typical tactile keyboard that they use on most of their gaming laptops it is nice to work on it and it is very easy to get used to it as well it has an rgb backlight which would be great for a gaming laptop but it doesn t really match an elegant creator laptop at least in my opinion i guess it s good to have an option to set it to any single color that you but personally i would have been fine with it just being white when it comes to the touchpad it is completely fine as well it is pretty large it is pretty smooth and it has an excellent wrist rejection which is very important because most of the time your right hand will cover it while you re typing but let s see that performance every thin high performance laptop needs to find a good balance between performance thermals and noise which is not that easy when you have a high power i9 12900h in a small chassis this one and every manufacturer has a different way of finding this balance now what msi did here is that they let the chip use quite a lot of power and run quite hot for a short while before tuning down the power of the cpu so in performance profile the cpu will use about 115 watts once you set it to work and hit 96 to 97 degrees but after a minute or so it drops down to 70 watts and it cools down so if we look at the benchmarks that don t take too long to complete the z17 is all the way up there with the fastest laptops on the market but if a task takes a little bit longer the 10 minute cinebench r23 benchmark for example it will drop down in the charts now keep in mind that even at 70 watts the i 912 900h is still one of the fastest cpus in the market and it will handle pretty much anything really well but it also means that it s better suited for tasks that require a lot of cpu power for a short while so if you are someone that spends a ton of time exporting long video projects you might want to reconsider if going thinner is worth it for you or not now during longer workloads the cpu stays at acceptable temperatures but it is a bit noisy standing at almost 47 decibels now if you want even better thermals msi offers a cooler boost setting but that makes the laptop so obnoxiously loud that i just cannot really recommend that unless you re really sitting alone and using a good headset if you want something quieter there is a balance mode as well which drops to a very nice and comfortable 35

review_summary: when you re looking at high end productivity laptops the two models that most people go for are the apple macbook pro 16 and the dell xps 17 because those are the laptops that offer some of the best specs on the market combined with the best build quality and they also carry a pretty high end price tag to match that but there are also other laptops on the market that are worth checking out too and today i m going to talk about this creator z17 which is a model that msi made to fit this top tier segment as well so it is made of metal it is pretty thin but it still holds the fastest hardware the money can buy and yes you will need a lot of money for this one as well so let s see how it performs and if it s worth getting at all let s begin 5 kilos without the charger on the left side we have the power connector two thunderbolt ports with one of them allowing usb charging as well and there is a headphone jack and on the right side there is an sd card reader a 10 gigabit usb type a port and an hdmi 2 1 port that supports 4k 120 or 8k 60 monitors now ideally it would have been nice to see a wired network connection but other than that i would say it s a pretty reasonable setup now it is very easy to open it up with one hand and if you go beyond 100 degrees or so it lifts up the bottom of the laptop which gives it a bit of an extra airflow now the build
